signatures.label: SELECT column1, column2 FROM table_name WHERE condition

documentation.value: Retrieves rows from Amazon Redshift tables. Optimized for columnar storage and massively parallel processing.
The columns to retrieve. Use * for all columns.
The table or view to query.
Optional WHERE clause for filtering.

signatures.label: COPY table_name FROM 's3://bucket/path' IAM_ROLE 'arn:aws:iam::role' FORMAT AS format

documentation.value: Loads data from Amazon S3 into a Redshift table. The most efficient way to load large datasets.
The target table to load data into.
The S3 path containing the source data files.
The ARN of the IAM role with S3 read permissions.
The data format: CSV, JSON, AVRO, PARQUET, ORC, DELIMITER.

signatures.label: UNLOAD ('select_statement') TO 's3://bucket/path' IAM_ROLE 'arn:aws:iam::role' FORMAT AS format

documentation.value: Unloads the result of a query to one or more files in Amazon S3.
The SELECT query whose results will be unloaded.
The S3 destination path for the output files.
The ARN of the IAM role with S3 write permissions.
The output format: CSV, PARQUET, JSON, DELIMITER.

signatures.label: CREATE TABLE table_name (column1 type, column2 type) DISTSTYLE style SORTKEY (column)

documentation.value: Creates a new Redshift table with optional distribution style and sort keys for query optimization.
The name of the table (optionally schema-qualified).
Column definitions. Redshift types: SMALLINT, INT, BIGINT, DECIMAL, REAL, DOUBLE, BOOLEAN, CHAR, VARCHAR, DATE, TIMESTAMP.
Distribution style: EVEN, KEY, ALL, or AUTO.
The sort key column(s) for optimizing query performance. Use COMPOUND or INTERLEAVED.

signatures.label: ALTER TABLE table_name ADD COLUMN column_name type

documentation.value: Modifies an existing table in Redshift. Supports ADD/DROP COLUMN, RENAME, owner changes.
The table to alter.
The new column name.
The data type of the new column.

signatures.label: VACUUM [FULL | SORT ONLY | DELETE ONLY | REINDEX] [table_name] [TO threshold PERCENT]

documentation.value: Reclaims disk space and resorts rows. Run after large DELETE or UPDATE operations.

parameters.label: FULL | SORT ONLY | DELETE ONLY | REINDEX

documentation.value: FULL: reclaims space and resorts. SORT ONLY: resorts only. DELETE ONLY: reclaims space only. REINDEX: reinitializes interleaved sort keys.
Optional. Specific table to vacuum. Without it, all tables are vacuumed.
Optional. The minimum percentage of rows to reclaim.
Updates table statistics for the query optimizer. Run after significant data changes.
Optional. The specific table to analyze. Without it, all tables are analyzed.
Optional. Specific columns to analyze.

signatures.label: GRANT privilege ON object TO user_or_group

documentation.value: Grants access privileges on database objects to users, groups, or roles.
The privilege: SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, ALL, EXECUTE, USAGE, CREATE.
The database object (TABLE name, SCHEMA name, DATABASE name, FUNCTION name).
The grantee: a user name, GROUP group_name, or PUBLIC.

signatures.label: REVOKE privilege ON object FROM user_or_group

documentation.value: Removes access privileges on database objects from users, groups, or roles.
The privilege to revoke: SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, ALL, EXECUTE, USAGE, CREATE.
The database object.
The user, group, or PUBLIC to revoke from.

signatures.label: SELECT * FROM STL_QUERY WHERE userid = user_id ORDER BY starttime DESC

documentation.value: System table that logs query information. Useful for monitoring query history and performance.
Filter by user ID.
The timestamp when the query started.
System view that provides table storage and design information including distribution style, sort keys, and compression.
Filter by schema name.
